title: Fix: setting the container name to the image
body:
In commit [47ac96155f1573e](https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/commit/47ac96155f1573e1bbf7804461f6850ff67e8747) the image name that is used for setting the container name is taken from the resolved image unless it is empty.

The resolved image has the `Names` field and right now the first name is taken. However, when the image is a tagged image, the container name will end up using the original name instead of the given one.

For example:
```sh
$ buildah tag busybox busybox1
$ buildah from busybox1
```

Will set the name of the container as `busybox-working-container` while it was expected to be `busybox1-working-container`.

This patch fixes this particular issue.
fixes #396

comments.body:
The code changes LGTM and it tests out well.  I do have a few questions about the test though.  Can that be moved into the tests directory and is it run automatically with each CI?  Our other tests are all bats based and I'd prefer to keep the test in the same format if possible.  @nalind WDYT?

The test that you came up with does cover the cases that need to be tested though.
I am fine with the unit test, but I would like to see at least one test that uses actual storage.  BTW CRI-O and Buildah are moving away from bats testing, we should probably follow suit.
@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat you got it. Added a test to `from.bats`.

:pushpin: Commit c72eee6 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan c72eee640f1b5bf4a17ec34d3c2c5ae5943cf4e9 -->
